docs.title: Bonded-Particle Model for Magneto-Elastic Rods
docs.text: ⚠️ This site is still under development.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Flexible rods undergoing large deformations are ubiquitous in science and engineering — from biological cilia and magnetic filaments to surgical catheters and soft robots. Simulating these systems requires a framework that naturally handles geometric nonlinearity, contact, and coupling to external fields and fluids. Bonded-particle models (BPMs) treat solids as collections of discrete particles connected by deformable bonds, and fit naturally within the discrete element method (DEM) — a general, scalable framework with modular multiphysics capabilities. Despite this versatility, BPMs remain underutilized for slender elastic rods. Here, we develop a co-rotational BPM formulation for slender rods, implement it in LAMMPS, and demonstrate its broad applicability across mechanics, magnetics, and fluid–structure interaction.

Extreme twisting & plectoneme formation Fluid pumping by a magnetic cilia array Helical rod with dipole–dipole interactions

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
This page provides the implementation details and code for the work described in:

Alkuino, G., Clemmer, J. T., Santangelo, C. D., & Zhang, T. (2026). Bonded-particle model for magneto-elastic rods. arXiv:2603.27279.

@article{alkuino_bpm_2026,
  author  = {Alkuino, Gabriel and Clemmer, Joel T. and
             Santangelo, Christian D. and Zhang, Teng},
  title   = {Bonded-particle model for magneto-elastic rods},
  journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:2603.27279},
  year    = {2026}
}

Please also cite LAMMPS if you use the code.

docs.title: Build Instructions
docs.text:
The model will be included in the BPM package starting from LAMMPS version (TBD). In the meantime, it is available on the develop branch of the LAMMPS repository (PR#4945).

To run all the examples on this site, build LAMMPS with these packages:

  • BPM — Bonded-particle models
  • DIPOLE — Dipole–dipole interactions for magnetic particles
  • GRANULAR — Granular pair styles for contact mechanics
  • LEPTON — String-expression-based potentials, used by fix efield/lepton for non-uniform fields
  • MOLECULE — Molecule IDs for grouping particles

For fluid–structure interaction examples, the custom CoupLB package is also required.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The model can be used by invoking the bond style bpm/rotational.

The bond style requires atom_style bpm/sphere and fix nve/bpm/sphere for time integration. For magnetic examples, the dipole sub-style must be added via atom_style hybrid. The update dipole keyword/value pair must be added to fix nve/bpm/sphere to use the magnetic model described in this work.

Bonds can be created using create_bonds or defined directly in the data file. The bond style and coefficients are specified as:

bond_style bpm/rotational <keyword/values>
bond_coeff type Kr Ks Kt Kb Fr_c Fs_c Tt_c Tb_c gr gs gt gb

The optional settings are:

Keyword Values Description Default frame average, particle average uses a symmetric central frame; particle uses one particle as reference average damping derivative, dem derivative damps strain rates; dem damps relative velocities derivative break yes, no Whether bonds can break during a run yes normalize yes, no Normalizes radial and shear forces by \(r_0\) no smooth yes, no Smoothly interpolates forces to zero near breaking yes

For each bond type, 12 coefficients must be defined, in order:

Coefficient Description \(K_r\), \(K_s\), \(K_t\), \(K_b\) Radial, shear, twist, and bend stiffnesses \(f_{r,c}\), \(f_{s,c}\), \(\tau_{t,c}\), \(\tau_{b,c}\) Critical forces/torques for bond breaking \(\gamma_r\), \(\gamma_s\), \(\gamma_t\), \(\gamma_b\) Damping coefficients for each mode

Note

The method described in the paper uses frame average and damping derivative (both defaults). All examples use break no and smooth no, with one bond type since each BPM segment is identical. With break no, the critical force/torque values are ignored.

For more information, see the How to BPM page.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
All examples are self-contained; the simplest is Demo: Cantilever Beams. The remaining examples assume familiarity with the basics covered there.

Demo: Cantilever Beams Cantilever beam under static and dynamic loading Extreme Twisting & Plectoneme Formation Straight and curved rods under extreme twisting with self-contact Magnetic Beams (under construction) Magnetized beams in uniform and constant-gradient magnetic fields Magnetic Helical Rods (under construction) Magnetized helical rods with dipole–dipole interactions and mechanical hysteresis FSI: Oscillatory Flow (under construction) Elastic filaments in oscillatory channel flow FSI: Magnetic Cilia (under construction) Fluid pumping by a cilia array with metachronal magnetization pattern

docs.text: • Unit Systems

docs.title: Demo: Cantilever Beams
docs.text:
Note

This demo assumes familiarity with running LAMMPS simulations (input scripts, data files, fixes, and computes). It is aimed at users experienced with molecular dynamics who want to learn the BPM workflow for simulating elastic rods.

This demo walks through the dynamic cantilever beam simulation in SI Sec. S3 based on the example from Le et al. 2011. By the end, you'll learn:

  • How to generate a data file with Python
  • How to define the BPM parameters
  • How to set up a simple simulation

Additionally, the scripts for the static loading examples are provided at the end.

docs.title: Cantilever beam under dynamic loading
docs.text:
A rectangular beam with one end clamped has its free end subject to a sinusoidal transverse force \(P(t) = P_0 \sin(\omega t)\), where \(P_0 = 10\) MN and \(\omega = 50\) rad/s. The following table lists the parameters.

Parameter Value Beam geometry (\(L \times w \times h\)) \(10\) m \(\times\) \(0.5\) m \(\times\) \(0.25\) m Young's modulus \(E\) 210 GPa Poisson's ratio \(\nu\) 0.3 Density \(\rho\) 7850 kg/m\(^3\) Tip force amplitude \(P_0\) 10 MN Angular frequency \(\omega\) 50 rad/s

docs.title: Part 1: Data File Generation
docs.text:
Although it is possible to create the data file within LAMMPS, especially for simple 1D systems, we prefer to do this using a Python script for flexibility.

For a straight beam, atoms are placed at evenly spaced positions along the x-axis with sequential IDs. Bonds connect consecutive atom pairs (1–2, 2–3, ..., N–N+1), giving \(N\) bonds and \(N+1\) atoms. A Python script is provided below.

create_beam.py ⬇ Download 
import numpy as np

N = 64  # number of bonds

# Units: SI

# Physical parameters
L = 10.
w = 0.5
h = 0.25
rho = 7850.

# Discretization
x = np.linspace(0, L, N+1)
dx = L/N

diam = h                    # set atom diameter to h; not required
mass = rho * dx * w * h     # mass of each bond...
m = mass * np.ones(N+1)    
m[0] = m[-1] = 0.5 * mass   # ...evenly split between the atoms
dens = m / (np.pi * diam**3 / 6)

with open('beam-{}.lam'.format(int(N)), 'w') as file:
    file.write('LAMMPS DATA FILE \n\n')

    file.write(str(N+1) + ' atoms \n')
    file.write(str(N) + ' bonds \n\n')

    file.write('1 atom types \n')
    file.write('1 bond types \n\n')

    # Create box
    file.write('%f %f xlo xhi \n'%(-1, 11))
    file.write('%f %f ylo yhi \n'%(-11, 11))
    file.write('%f %f zlo zhi \n\n'%(-0.1, 0.1))

    # atom_style bpm/sphere
    file.write('Atoms \n\n')
    for k in range(N+1):
        # atom-id atom-type molid diameter density x y z
        file.write('%i 1 1 %f %f %f 0 0 \n'%(k+1, diam, dens[k], x[k]))
    file.write('\n')

    file.write('Bonds \n\n')
    for k in range(N):
        file.write('%i %i %i %i \n'%(k+1, 1, k+1, k+2))
    file.write('\n')

Important details:

  • atom_style bpm/sphere uses per-atom mass set via density and not per-type mass. The effective density is back-calculated from the target mass and the chosen diameter.
  • Mass: each bond with rest length \(\ell_0 = L/N\) represents a rectangular segment of volume \(\ell_0 \times w \times h\). The masses are split between the two atoms forming the bond.
  • Diameter: the atom diameter is a geometric parameter for LAMMPS, not the physical rod diameter. Here we set it to \(h = 0.25\) m.

docs.title: Part 2: BPM Parameters
docs.text:
Note

Although the BPM assumes cylindrical bond segments, the four spring constants can be defined independently. For planar bending without twisting, \(K_r\), \(K_s\), and \(K_b\) can be consistently defined for any cross-section. The twist stiffness \(K_t\) does not affect the result in 2D.

For a rectangular beam with width \(w\) and depth \(h\):

  • Area: \(A = wh\)
  • Second moment of area: \(I = wh^3/12\)
  • Polar second moment: \(J = wh(w^2 + h^2)/12\)

The four spring constants are as follows:

Stiffness Formula Role \(K_r\) \(EA / \ell_0\) Axial stretching \(K_s\) \(12EI / \ell_0^3\) Transverse shear \(K_t\) \(GJ / \ell_0\) Twisting \(K_b\) \(EI / \ell_0\) Bending

The \(K_s\) and \(K_b\) formulas follow from Euler–Bernoulli beam theory (Chen et al. 2022). All damping coefficients are set to zero since this is a fully dynamic, undamped simulation to test the natural frequency response.

docs.title: Atom diameter and moment of inertia
docs.text:
The atom diameter is set to \(h\) in the data file. LAMMPS computes the moment of inertia of each bpm/sphere atom as \(I_\text{sphere} = \tfrac{1}{10} m d^2\). With \(d = h\), this is close enough to the Timoshenko rotary inertia \(\tfrac{1}{12} m h^2\).

An alternative is to adjust the density and the diameter of the atom to produce the desired mass and moment of inertia.

Note

The fix nve/bpm/sphere also has a disc keyword, which only changes the default moment of inertia formula. Since the diameter and density in the data file assume spherical atoms, do not add the disc keyword.

docs.title: Part 3: Simulation Setup
docs.text: The left and right groups are defined using regions that only capture the leftmost and rightmost atoms, respectively (assuming \(N < 100\)). The clamped boundary condition on the left end is imposed by excluding left from time integration. This means the leftmost atom's position and orientation are never updated. The sinusoidal tip force is applied to the rightmost atom via fix addforce. The total simulation time is \(1.5\) s.

docs.title: Timestep selection
docs.text:
The timestep \(\Delta t\) for explicit time integration must be smaller than the critical timestep set by the highest-frequency mode. For translational modes, \(\omega = \sqrt{k/m}\); for rotational modes, \(\omega = \sqrt{k/I_\text{sphere}}\).

Assuming \(N = 100\) (\(\ell_0 = 0.1\) m, \(m \approx 98\) kg, \(I \approx 0.61\) kg m\(^2\)):

Mode Stiffness \(\omega\) [rad/s] \(\Delta t_c = 2/\omega\) [s] Radial \(K_r = EA/\ell_0 \approx 2.6 \times 10^{11}\) \(5.2 \times 10^{4}\) \(3.9 \times 10^{-5}\) Shear \(K_s = 12EI/\ell_0^3 \approx 1.6 \times 10^{12}\) \(1.3 \times 10^{5}\) \(1.5 \times 10^{-5}\) Twist \(K_t = GJ/\ell_0 \approx 2.6 \times 10^{9}\) \(6.5 \times 10^{4}\) \(3.1 \times 10^{-5}\) Bend \(K_b = EI/\ell_0 \approx 1.4 \times 10^{9}\) \(4.7 \times 10^{4}\) \(4.2 \times 10^{-5}\)

The shear spring is the stiffest, giving \(\Delta t_c \approx 1.5 \times 10^{-5}\) s. In molecular dynamics, a good rule of thumb is to set \(\Delta t < 0.1 \Delta t_c\). Here, we use \(\Delta t = 10^{-8}\) s, which is well below the threshold.

docs.title: Notes:
docs.text:
• special_bonds, newton and comm_modify settings documented in How to BPM.
  • Pair style: pair_style zero — no contact needed for this problem
  • fix addforce to apply the loading
  • Time integration: fix nve/bpm/sphere (no damping, no dipole update)
  • Output: tip deflection \(\delta_x / L\) and \(\delta_y / L\) printed to file at regular intervals. The tip displacement is tracked using compute reduce ave on the right group.

The LAMMPS simulation script is provided below.

bend-dynamic-point-load.in ⬇ Download 
# =========================================================================
# Cantilever beam under sinusoidal point load on free end
# 
# Run: lmp -var NBONDS <INPUT> -in bend-dynamic-point-load.in
# =========================================================================

variable    N       equal NBONDS

# Beam geometry
variable    L       equal 10.
variable    d0      equal $(v_L/v_N)
variable    w       equal 0.5
variable    h       equal 0.25

# Rectangular cross-section
variable    A       equal v_w*v_h
variable    I       equal v_w*v_h^3/12
variable    J       equal v_w*v_h/12*(v_w^2+v_h^2)

# Material properties
variable    E       equal 210e9
variable    nu      equal 0.3
variable    G       equal "v_E/(2*(1+v_nu))"

# Loading
variable    P0      equal 10e6
variable    omega   equal 50

# =========================================================================
# Initialization
# =========================================================================

units           si
dimension       3
boundary        f f f
processors      * 1 1

atom_style      bpm/sphere
special_bonds   lj 0.0 1.0 1.0 coul 1.0 1.0 1.0
newton          on off

neighbor        0.5 bin
neigh_modify    once yes
comm_modify     mode single cutoff $(2*v_d0) vel yes

read_data       beam-${N}.lam

# =========================================================================
# Groups
# =========================================================================

region          left block INF 0.1 INF INF INF INF
region          right block 9.9 INF INF INF INF INF
group           left region left
group           right region right
group           free subtract all left

# =========================================================================
# BPM bond style
# =========================================================================

variable        Kr equal v_E*v_A/v_d0
variable        Ks equal 12*v_E*v_I/v_d0^3
variable        Kt equal v_G*v_J/v_d0
variable        Kb equal v_E*v_I/v_d0

bond_style      bpm/rotational smooth no break no
bond_coeff      1 ${Kr} ${Ks} ${Kt} ${Kb} &
                -1 -1 -1 -1 &
                0 0 0 0

pair_style      zero $(2*v_d0)
pair_coeff      * * $(2*v_d0)

# =========================================================================
# Simulation time and output
# =========================================================================

variable        timestep equal 1e-8
variable        runtime  equal 1.5
variable        nsteps   equal v_runtime/v_timestep
variable        time     equal step*v_timestep

timestep        ${timestep}

compute         tx right reduce ave x
compute         ty right reduce ave y
variable        dx equal "c_tx/v_L-1"
variable        dy equal "c_ty/v_L"

compute         mytemp all temp/sphere
compute         erot all erotate/sphere

thermo          $(v_nsteps/1000)
thermo_style    custom step temp v_time v_dx v_dy
thermo_modify   temp mytemp

dump            1 all custom $(v_nsteps/100) ./bend-point-N${N}.dump &
                id x y z fx fy fz

fix             prnt all print $(v_nsteps/1000) &
                "${time} ${dx} ${dy}" &
                file deflection-N${N}.txt screen no &
                title "t dx/L dy/L"

# =========================================================================
# Dynamics
# =========================================================================

fix             nve free nve/bpm/sphere

variable        p equal v_P0*sin(v_omega*step*v_timestep)
fix             adf right addforce 0 v_p 0
fix_modify      adf energy no

run             ${nsteps}

docs.title: Cantilever beam under static loading
docs.text: These examples use a different beam geometry: a cylindrical rod with \(L = 100\), \(d = 1\), \(E = 1\), \(\nu = 0.5\) in dimensionless units (units lj). The applied loads are chosen such that the maximum tip deflection is 10% of the beam length, and the simulated shapes are compared against the exact Euler–Bernoulli deflection curves (Hibbeler 2018).

docs.title: Key differences from the dynamic example:
docs.text:
• Damping: fix langevin is used to reach quasi-static equilibrium. The load is first ramped from zero, then held constant while the beam relaxes.
  • Loading: point load (fix addforce on right) or uniform load (fix addforce on all free atoms, with total load distributed evenly).
  • Energy verification: the scripts compare the LAMMPS bond energy against the theoretical strain energy (\(U = P^2 L^3 / (6EI)\) for point load; \(U = w^2 L^5 / (40EI)\) for uniform load).
create_beam_for_static.py ⬇ Download 
import numpy as np

N = 8  # number of bonds

# Dimensionless units
L = 100.
dx = L / N

with open('beam-{}.lam'.format(int(N)), 'w') as file:
    file.write('LAMMPS DATA FILE \n\n')

    file.write(str(N+1) + ' atoms \n')
    file.write(str(N) + ' bonds \n\n')

    file.write('1 atom types \n')
    file.write('1 bond types \n\n')

    file.write('%f %f xlo xhi \n' % (-1, 101))
    file.write('%f %f ylo yhi \n' % (-101, 101))
    file.write('%f %f zlo zhi \n\n' % (-0.1, 0.1))

    # atom_style bpm/sphere
    file.write('Atoms \n\n')
    x = 0.
    for k in range(N+1):
        # atom-id atom-type molid diameter density x y z
        file.write('%i 1 1 1.0 1.0 %f 0 0 \n' % (k+1, x))
        x += dx
    file.write('\n')

    file.write('Bonds \n\n')
    for k in range(N):
        file.write('%i %i %i %i \n' % (k+1, 1, k+1, k+2))
    file.write('\n')
bend-static-point-load.in ⬇ Download 
# =========================================================================
# Cantilever beam under static point load on free end
# 
# Run: lmp -var NBONDS <INPUT> -in bend-static-point-load.in
# =========================================================================

variable    N       equal NBONDS

# Beam geometry (dimensionless)
variable    L       equal 100
variable    diam    equal 1
variable    d0      equal $(v_L/v_N)

# Cylindrical cross-section
variable    A       equal (PI/4)*v_diam^2
variable    I       equal (PI/64)*v_diam^4
variable    J       equal (PI/32)*v_diam^4

# Material properties
variable    E       equal 1
variable    nu      equal 0.5
variable    G       equal "v_E/(2*(1+v_nu))"

# Load chosen such that max tip deflection = 10% of L
variable    dymax   equal "-0.1*v_L"
variable    p       equal $(3*v_E*v_I*v_dymax/v_L^3)

# =========================================================================
# Initialization
# =========================================================================

units           lj
dimension       3
boundary        f f f
processors      * 1 1

atom_style      bpm/sphere
special_bonds   lj 0.0 1.0 1.0 coul 1.0 1.0 1.0
newton          on off

neighbor        0.5 bin
neigh_modify    once yes
comm_modify     mode single cutoff $(2*v_d0) vel yes

read_data       beam-${N}.lam

change_box      all x final -500 500 y final -500 500 z final -500 500

# =========================================================================
# Groups
# =========================================================================

region          left block INF 0.1 INF INF INF INF
region          right block 99.9 INF INF INF INF INF
group           left region left
group           right region right
group           free subtract all left

# =========================================================================
# BPM bond style
# =========================================================================

variable        Kr equal v_E*v_A/v_d0
variable        Ks equal 12*v_E*v_I/v_d0^3
variable        Kt equal v_G*v_J/v_d0
variable        Kb equal v_E*v_I/v_d0

bond_style      bpm/rotational smooth no break no
bond_coeff      1 ${Kr} ${Ks} ${Kt} ${Kb} &
                -1 -1 -1 -1 &
                1e-2 1e-2 1e-2 1e-2

pair_style      zero $(2*v_d0)
pair_coeff      * * $(2*v_d0)

# =========================================================================
# Computes and output
# =========================================================================

timestep        0.01

compute         delx right reduce ave x
compute         dely right reduce ave y
variable        dx equal "c_delx/v_L-1"
variable        dy equal "c_dely/v_L"

# Theoretical deflection curve (Euler-Bernoulli)
variable        ytheo atom "v_p*x^2/(6*v_E*v_I)*(3*v_L-x)"
variable        ydiff atom "y-v_ytheo"
compute         maxdiffy all reduce maxabs v_ydiff

# Energy
variable        Wext equal v_p*c_dely
variable        Uf_theo equal $(v_L^3*v_p^2/(6*v_E*v_I))

compute         mytemp all temp/sphere
compute         erot all erotate/sphere
variable        U equal "pe"
variable        Uerr equal abs(v_U-v_Uf_theo)/v_Uf_theo

thermo          1000000
thermo_style    custom step temp ke c_erot pe v_U v_Uf_theo v_Uerr v_Wext v_dy c_maxdiffy
thermo_modify   temp mytemp norm no

dump            1 all custom 1000000 ./bend-point-N${N}.dump &
                id x y z

# =========================================================================
# Stage 1: Ramp load
# =========================================================================

fix             nve free nve/bpm/sphere

variable        fy equal v_p*ramp(0,1)
fix             adf right addforce 0 v_fy 0
fix_modify      adf energy no

run             50000000

# =========================================================================
# Stage 2: Relax under constant load
# =========================================================================

fix             lan free langevin 0 0 1000 83715463 zero yes omega yes

variable        fy equal v_p
fix             adf right addforce 0 v_fy 0
fix_modify      adf energy no

run             50000000

# =========================================================================
# Post-processing
# =========================================================================

variable        Wextf_theo equal v_p*v_dymax

variable        fddy atom "v_p/(v_E*v_I)*(v_L-x)"
variable        Uf_atom atom "0.5*v_E*v_I*(v_fddy)^2*v_d0"
compute         Uf_discrete all reduce sum v_Uf_atom
run             0

print "======================================================================"
print "External work:       W = ${Wext}"
print "Theoretical:         W = ${Wextf_theo}"
print "======================================================================"
print "Elastic energy:      U = ${Uf_theo}"
print "Discrete approx:     U = $(c_Uf_discrete)"
print "======================================================================"

dump            final all custom 1 ./bend-point-N${N}-final.dump &
                id x y z
run             0
bend-static-uniform-load.in ⬇ Download 
# =========================================================================
# Cantilever beam under static uniform load
# 
# Run: lmp -var NBONDS <INPUT> -in bend-static-uniform-load.in
# =========================================================================

variable    N       equal NBONDS

# Beam geometry (dimensionless)
variable    L       equal 100
variable    diam    equal 1
variable    d0      equal $(v_L/v_N)

# Cylindrical cross-section
variable    A       equal (PI/4)*v_diam^2
variable    I       equal (PI/64)*v_diam^4
variable    J       equal (PI/32)*v_diam^4

# Material properties
variable    E       equal 1
variable    nu      equal 0.5
variable    G       equal "v_E/(2*(1+v_nu))"

# Load chosen such that max tip deflection = 10% of L
variable    dymax   equal "-0.1*v_L"
variable    w       equal 8*v_E*v_I*v_dymax/v_L^4

# =========================================================================
# Initialization
# =========================================================================

units           lj
dimension       3
boundary        f f f
processors      * 1 1

atom_style      bpm/sphere
special_bonds   lj 0.0 1.0 1.0 coul 1.0 1.0 1.0
newton          on off

neighbor        0.5 bin
neigh_modify    once yes
comm_modify     mode single cutoff $(2*v_d0) vel yes

read_data       beam-${N}.lam

change_box      all x final -500 500 y final -500 500 z final -500 500

# =========================================================================
# Groups
# =========================================================================

region          left block INF 0.1 INF INF INF INF
region          right block 99.9 INF INF INF INF INF
group           left region left
group           right region right
group           free subtract all left

# =========================================================================
# BPM bond style
# =========================================================================

variable        Kr equal v_E*v_A/v_d0
variable        Ks equal 12*v_E*v_I/v_d0^3
variable        Kt equal v_G*v_J/v_d0
variable        Kb equal v_E*v_I/v_d0

bond_style      bpm/rotational smooth no break no
bond_coeff      1 ${Kr} ${Ks} ${Kt} ${Kb} &
                -1 -1 -1 -1 &
                1e-2 1e-2 1e-2 1e-2

pair_style      zero $(2*v_d0)
pair_coeff      * * $(2*v_d0)

# =========================================================================
# Computes and output
# =========================================================================

timestep        0.01

compute         delx right reduce ave x
compute         dely right reduce ave y
variable        dx equal "c_delx/v_L-1"
variable        dy equal "c_dely/v_L"

# Distribute total load to free atoms
variable        Fy equal v_w*v_L/count(free)

# Theoretical deflection curve (Euler-Bernoulli)
variable        ytheo atom "v_w*x^2/(24*v_E*v_I)*(6*v_L^2-4*v_L*x+x^2)"
variable        ydiff atom "y-v_ytheo"
compute         maxdiffy all reduce maxabs v_ydiff

# Energy
variable        Wexta atom v_w*v_d0*y
compute         Wext all reduce sum v_Wexta
variable        Uf_theo equal $(v_L^5*v_w^2/(40*v_E*v_I))

compute         mytemp all temp/sphere
compute         erot all erotate/sphere
variable        U equal "pe"
variable        Uerr equal abs(v_U-v_Uf_theo)/v_Uf_theo

thermo          1000000
thermo_style    custom step temp ke c_erot pe v_U v_Uf_theo v_Uerr c_Wext v_dy c_maxdiffy
thermo_modify   temp mytemp norm no

dump            1 all custom 1000000 ./bend-uniform-N${N}.dump &
                id x y z

# =========================================================================
# Stage 1: Ramp load
# =========================================================================

fix             nve free nve/bpm/sphere

variable        fy equal v_Fy*ramp(0,1)
fix             adf free addforce 0 v_fy 0
fix_modify      adf energy no

run             50000000

# =========================================================================
# Stage 2: Relax under constant load
# =========================================================================

fix             lan free langevin 0 0 1000 83715463 zero yes omega yes

variable        fy equal v_Fy
fix             adf free addforce 0 v_fy 0
fix_modify      adf energy no

run             50000000

# =========================================================================
# Post-processing
# =========================================================================

variable        Wexta_theo atom v_w*v_d0*v_ytheo
compute         Wextf_theo all reduce sum v_Wexta_theo

variable        fddy atom "v_w/(v_E*v_I)*(0.5*v_L^2-v_L*x+0.5*x^2)"
variable        Uf_atom atom "0.5*v_E*v_I*(v_fddy)^2*v_d0"
compute         Uf_discrete all reduce sum v_Uf_atom
run             0

print "======================================================================"
print "External work:       W = $(c_Wext)"
print "Theoretical:         W = $(c_Wextf_theo)"
print "======================================================================"
print "Elastic energy:      U = ${Uf_theo}"
print "Discrete approx:     U = $(c_Uf_discrete)"
print "======================================================================"

dump            final all custom 1 ./bend-uniform-N${N}-final.dump &
                id x y z
run             0

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Le, T.-N., Battini, J.-M., Hjiaj, M. (2011). Efficient formulation for dynamics of corotational 2D beams. Computational Mechanics, 48(2), 153–161. doi:10.1007/s00466-011-0585-6
  • Chen, X., Peng, D., Morrissey, J.P., Ooi, J.Y. (2022). A comparative assessment and unification of bond models in DEM simulations. Granular Matter, 24(1), 29. doi:10.1007/s10035-021-01187-2
  • Hibbeler, R.C. (2018). Mechanics of Materials. Pearson.

← Back to Examples

docs.title: Extreme Twisting & Plectoneme Formation
docs.text:
Sec. 3.1 — Based on Lazarus et al. (2013)

A heavy elastic rod is axially compressed until it buckles under gravity, then twisted up to 9 full rotations. The simulation captures writhing instabilities and plectoneme formation with self-contact via Hertz–Mindlin interactions. Two cases are considered: an initially straight rod and a naturally curved rod.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Value Rod length \(L\) 300 mm Rod diameter \(d\) 3.1 mm Density \(\rho\) 1.2 g/cm\(^3\) Young's modulus \(E\) 1.3 MPa Shear modulus \(G\) 433 kPa Number of bonds \(N\) 300 (bond length \(\ell_0 \approx 1\) mm) Curvature (curved rod) \(\kappa = 44.84\) m\(^{-1}\) (2.14 coils)

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: Both cases share the same material properties and boundary conditions. The left end is clamped (position and orientation fixed); the right end is displaced and rotated using fix move.

docs.title: Straight rod (twist-straight.in)
docs.text:
1. Compress: the right end is displaced by 80 mm at a constant velocity.
  2. Relax: the rod equilibrates under Langevin dynamics with gravity.
  3. Twist: the right end is rotated up to 9 full turns at a constant rate using fix move rotate.

docs.title: Curved rod (twist-curved.in)
docs.text:
The rod is initialized in its stress-free coiled configuration and bonds are created in this state. An additional stage precedes the compression:

  1. Straighten: a pure bending moment \(M = \kappa EI\) is applied at the free end via fix addtorque/atom to uncoil the rod. Motion is constrained to 2D using fix setforce NULL NULL 0. Contact and gravity are disabled during this stage.
  2. Compress: similar to the straight rod.
  3. Relax: similar to the straight rod.
  4. Twist: similar to the straight rod, with an additional frictionless wall at the rotating end (see below).

Atom types and pair style. The left and right end atoms are assigned different atom types (set group left type 2, set group right type 3). A pair_style hybrid zero ... granular is used so that during straightening (Stage 0), no granular contact is active — the coiled rod overlaps itself and contact would interfere with the uncoiling. The pair_coeff 2 3 granular ... assignment exists only to satisfy LAMMPS's requirement that every sub-style in a hybrid pair style is referenced by at least one pair_coeff; types 2 and 3 are at opposite ends of the rod and never contact each other. After straightening, type 1–1 granular contact is enabled for the compression and twist stages.

Wall at the rotating end. During twisting, the plectoneme loop can migrate toward the rotating end and slip off, causing the rod to untwist. A frictionless wall (fix wall/gran ... xplane 0 220) is placed near the rotating end to prevent this. The straight rod does not need this wall because its plectoneme forms farther from the end.

docs.title: Key LAMMPS settings
docs.text:
• Units: units lj with milli units (see Unit System)
  • Atom style: atom_style hybrid bpm/sphere dipole — a "dipole" is assigned to each atom to visualize the twist. Note that the dipole vector does not capture the full 3D rotation.
  • Bond style: bond_style bpm/rotational with smooth no, break no
  • Contact: pair_style granular with Hertz–Mindlin; atom diameter set to \(\approx 3 \ell_0\) for smooth contact surface; special_bonds lj 0 0 0 disables pair forces for bonded neighbors up to 3 bonds away. This is necessary because the atom diameter causes bonded neighbors to overlap.
  • Time integration: fix nve/bpm/sphere update dipole
  • Damping: fix langevin for quasi-static equilibration

Note

The atom diameter is independent of the rod diameter \(d\). The latter is only used to compute the BPM parameters.

docs.title: Data file generator
docs.text:
create_straight_rod.py ⬇ Download 
import numpy as np

N = 300  # number of bonds

# Units: (mm, mg, ms)

# Physical parameters
L = 300.
d = 3.1
rho = 1.2

# Discretization
x = np.linspace(0, L, N+1)

dx = L/N
A = np.pi * d**2 / 4
mass = rho * dx * A

diam = 3 * dx # atom diameter for contact != rod diameter
m = mass * np.ones(N+1)
m[0] = m[-1] = 0.5 * mass
dens = m / (np.pi * diam**3 / 6)

with open('rod-N{}.lam'.format(int(N)), 'w') as file:
    file.write('LAMMPS DATA FILE \n\n')

    file.write(str(N+1) + ' atoms \n')
    file.write(str(N) + ' bonds \n\n')

    file.write('1 atom types \n')
    file.write('1 bond types \n\n')

    # Create box
    file.write('%f %f xlo xhi \n'%(-0.1*L, 1.1*L))
    file.write('%f %f ylo yhi \n'%(-0.6*L, 0.6*L))
    file.write('%f %f zlo zhi \n\n'%(-0.6*L, 0.6*L))

    # atom_style hybrid bpm/sphere dipole
    file.write('Atoms \n\n')
    for k in range(N+1):
        # atom-id atom-type x y z molid diameter density q mux muy muz
        file.write('%i 1 %f 0 0 1 %f %f 0 0 1 0\n'%(k+1, x[k], diam, dens[k]))
    file.write('\n')

    file.write('Bonds \n\n')
    for k in range(N):
        file.write('%i %i %i %i \n'%(k+1, 1, k+1, k+2))
    file.write('\n')
create_curved_rod.py ⬇ Download 
import numpy as np

N = 300  # number of bonds

# Units: (mm, mg, ms)

# Physical parameters
L = 300.
d = 3.1
rho = 1.2

# Curvature
R = 1/44.84 * 1000
theta = L/R

# Discretization
t = np.linspace(0, theta, N+1)
x = R * np.cos(t-np.pi/2)
y = R * (1 + np.sin(t-np.pi/2))

dx = L/N
A = np.pi * d**2 / 4
mass = rho * dx * A

diam = 3 * dx # atom diameter for contact != rod diameter
m = mass * np.ones(N+1)
m[0] = m[-1] = 0.5 * mass
dens = m / (np.pi * diam**3 / 6)

with open('curved-rod-N{}.lam'.format(int(N)), 'w') as file:
    file.write('LAMMPS DATA FILE \n\n')

    file.write(str(N+1) + ' atoms \n')
    file.write(str(N) + ' bonds \n\n')

    file.write('3 atom types \n')
    file.write('1 bond types \n\n')

    # Create box
    file.write('%f %f xlo xhi \n'%(-1.1*L, 1.1*L))
    file.write('%f %f ylo yhi \n'%(-1.1*L, 1.1*L))
    file.write('%f %f zlo zhi \n\n'%(-0.6*L, 0.6*L))

    # atom_style hybrid bpm/sphere dipole
    file.write('Atoms \n\n')
    for k in range(N+1):
        # atom-id atom-type x y z molid diameter density q mux muy muz
        file.write('%i 1 %f %f 0 1 %f %f 0 0 1 0\n'%(k+1, x[k], y[k], diam, dens[k]))
    file.write('\n')

    file.write('Bonds \n\n')
    for k in range(N):
        file.write('%i %i %i %i \n'%(k+1, 1, k+1, k+2))
    file.write('\n')

docs.title: LAMMPS input scripts
docs.text:
twist-straight.in ⬇ Download 
# =========================================================================
# Twisting a pre-buckled straight rod
# =========================================================================

variable    N       equal 300

# Beam geometry (mm)
variable    L       equal 300
variable    l0      equal $(v_L/v_N)
variable    d       equal 3.1

# Cross-section properties
variable    A       equal (PI/4)*v_d^2
variable    I       equal (PI/64)*v_d^4
variable    J       equal (PI/32)*v_d^4

# Elastic moduli (kPa)
variable    E       equal 1300
variable    G       equal 433

# =========================================================================
# Initialization
# =========================================================================

units           lj
dimension       3
boundary        f f f
processors      * 1 1

atom_style      hybrid bpm/sphere dipole
special_bonds   lj 0.0 0.0 0.0 coul 1.0 1.0 1.0
newton          on off

neighbor        $(5*v_l0) bin
comm_modify     mode single cutoff $(5*v_l0) vel yes

read_data       rod-N${N}.lam

# =========================================================================
# Groups
# =========================================================================

group           l1 id 1
group           l2 id 2
group           r2 id $(v_N)
group           r1 id $(v_N+1)

group           left  union l1 l2
group           right union r1 r2
group           ends  union l1 r1
group           free  subtract all ends
group           mobile union free right

# =========================================================================
# BPM bond style
# =========================================================================

variable        Kr equal v_E*v_A/v_l0
variable        Ks equal 12*v_E*v_I/v_l0^3
variable        Kt equal v_G*v_J/v_l0
variable        Kb equal v_E*v_I/v_l0

bond_style      bpm/rotational smooth no break no overlay/pair no
bond_coeff      1 ${Kr} ${Ks} ${Kt} ${Kb} &
                -1 -1 -1 -1 &
                1e-4 1e-4 1e-4 1e-4

# =========================================================================
# Granular contact (Hertz-Mindlin)
# =========================================================================

# Made up values
variable        YoungsModulus    equal 10*v_E
variable        PoissonsRatio    equal 0.4
variable        FrictionCoeff    equal 0.1
variable        CoeffRestitution equal 0.7

pair_style      granular limit_damping yes
pair_coeff      * * hertz/material $(v_YoungsModulus) $(v_CoeffRestitution) $(v_PoissonsRatio) &
                tangential mindlin/force NULL 0.5 $(v_FrictionCoeff) &
                damping coeff_restitution

# =========================================================================
# Computes and output
# =========================================================================

compute         mytemp all temp/sphere
compute         kerot  all erotate/sphere

# Note: variable torque and compute torque/chunk only works for group of point particles
compute         ft l1 property/atom fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz
compute         f  l1 reduce sum c_ft[1] c_ft[2] c_ft[3]
compute         t  l1 reduce sum c_ft[4] c_ft[5] c_ft[6]

variable        fx equal c_f[1]
variable        tx equal c_t[1]

compute         xmax    all reduce max x
compute         xmin    all reduce min x
variable        dx      equal "c_xmax-c_xmin"
compute         quat    all property/atom quatw quati quatj quatk

compute         ymaxabs all reduce maxabs y
compute         zmaxabs all reduce maxabs z

variable        cycles equal 0

thermo          10000
thermo_style    custom step ke c_kerot pe v_cycles v_dx c_ymaxabs c_zmaxabs v_fx v_tx
thermo_modify   temp mytemp norm no

# =========================================================================
# Stage 1: Axial compression (80 mm)
# =========================================================================

dump            1 all custom 100000 ./compress-N${N}.dump &
                id diameter x y z mux muy muz fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz c_quat[*]

variable        timestep equal 1e-3
timestep        ${timestep}

variable        nsteps equal 5e6
variable        period equal v_timestep*v_nsteps
variable        vx     equal -80/(v_timestep*v_nsteps)

fix             1 free nve/bpm/sphere update dipole
fix             2 r1 move linear ${vx} 0 0
fix             3 free gravity 9.81e-3 vector 0 -1 0
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e3 12345 omega yes

run             $(v_nsteps)

# =========================================================================
# Stage 2: Relaxation
# =========================================================================

unfix           2
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e3 12345 omega yes

variable        nsteps equal 1e7
run             $(v_nsteps)

# write_restart   compressed-beam.restart

undump          1
unfix           1
unfix           3
unfix           4

# =========================================================================
# Stage 3: Twist (9 full rotations)
# =========================================================================

variable        timestep equal 1e-5
timestep        ${timestep}
reset_timestep  0

balance         1.2 shift x 10 1.1

dump            1 all custom 10000000 ./twist-N${N}.dump &
                id diameter x y z mux muy muz fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz c_quat[*]

variable        nturns       equal 9
variable        stepsperturn equal 1e9
variable        period       equal v_timestep*v_stepsperturn
variable        cycles       equal step/v_stepsperturn
variable        theta        equal 2*PI*v_cycles

bond_coeff      1 ${Kr} ${Ks} ${Kt} ${Kb} &
                -1 -1 -1 -1 &
                1e-2 1e-2 1e-2 1e-2

fix             1 free nve/bpm/sphere update dipole
fix             2 r1 move rotate 0 0 0 -1 0 0 $(v_period) update dipole
fix             3 free gravity 9.81e-3 vector 0 -1 0
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e2 12345 omega yes

fix             5 all print 1000000 &
                "${theta} $(c_ymaxabs) $(c_zmaxabs) ${tx}" &
                file twist-N${N}-theta-yzmaxabs-Tx.txt screen no &
                title "theta [rad], y_maxabs [mm], z_maxabs [mm], Tx [uJ]"

variable        i loop ${nturns}
label           loopstart
run             $(v_stepsperturn)
next            i
jump            SELF loopstart
twist-curved.in ⬇ Download 
# =========================================================================
# Twisting a pre-buckled naturally curved rod
# =========================================================================

variable    N       equal 300

# Beam geometry (mm)
variable    R       equal 1000/44.84
variable    L       equal 300
variable    l0      equal $(v_L/v_N)
variable    d       equal 3.1

# Cross-section properties
variable    A       equal (PI/4)*v_d^2
variable    I       equal (PI/64)*v_d^4
variable    J       equal (PI/32)*v_d^4

# Elastic moduli (kPa)
variable    E       equal 1300
variable    G       equal 433

# =========================================================================
# Initialization
# =========================================================================

units           lj
dimension       3
boundary        f f f
processors      * 1 1

atom_style      hybrid bpm/sphere dipole
special_bonds   lj 0.0 0.0 0.0 coul 1.0 1.0 1.0
newton          on off

neighbor        $(5*v_l0) bin
comm_modify     mode single cutoff $(5*v_l0) vel yes

read_data       curved-rod-N${N}.lam

# =========================================================================
# Groups
# =========================================================================

group           l1 id 1
group           l2 id 2
group           r2 id $(v_N)
group           r1 id $(v_N+1)

group           left  union l1 l2
group           right union r1 r2
group           ends  union l1 r1
group           free  subtract all ends
group           mobile union free right

set             group left type 2
set             group right type 3

# =========================================================================
# BPM bond style
# =========================================================================

variable        Kr equal v_E*v_A/v_l0
variable        Ks equal 12*v_E*v_I/v_l0^3
variable        Kt equal v_G*v_J/v_l0
variable        Kb equal v_E*v_I/v_l0

bond_style      bpm/rotational smooth no break no overlay/pair no
bond_coeff      1 ${Kr} ${Ks} ${Kt} ${Kb} &
                -1 -1 -1 -1 &
                1e-2 1e-2 1e-2 1e-2

# =========================================================================
# Granular contact (Hertz-Mindlin)
# =========================================================================

# Made up values
variable        YoungsModulus    equal 10*v_E
variable        PoissonsRatio    equal 0.4
variable        FrictionCoeff    equal 0.1
variable        CoeffRestitution equal 0.7

pair_style      hybrid zero $(5*v_l0) granular limit_damping yes
pair_coeff      * * zero
pair_coeff      2 3 granular hertz/material $(v_YoungsModulus) $(v_CoeffRestitution) $(v_PoissonsRatio) &
                tangential mindlin/force NULL 0.5 $(v_FrictionCoeff) &
                damping coeff_restitution

# =========================================================================
# Computes and output
# =========================================================================

compute         mytemp  all temp/sphere
compute         kerot   all erotate/sphere

# Note: variable torque and compute torque/chunk only works for group of point particles
compute         ft l1 property/atom fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz
compute         f  l1 reduce sum c_ft[1] c_ft[2] c_ft[3]
compute         t  l1 reduce sum c_ft[4] c_ft[5] c_ft[6]

variable        fx equal c_f[1]
variable        tx equal c_t[1]

compute         xmax    all reduce max x
compute         xmin    all reduce min x
variable        dx      equal "c_xmax-c_xmin"
compute         quat    all property/atom quatw quati quatj quatk

compute         ymaxabs all reduce maxabs y
compute         zmaxabs all reduce maxabs z

variable        cycles equal 0

thermo          10000
thermo_style    custom step ke c_kerot pe v_cycles v_dx c_ymaxabs c_zmaxabs v_fx v_tx
thermo_modify   temp mytemp norm no

# =========================================================================
# Stage 0: Straighten by applying end moment M = kappa * EI
#          (no gravity, no contact)
# =========================================================================

dump            1 all custom 100000 ./bend-N${N}.dump &
                id diameter x y z mux muy muz fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz c_quat[*]

variable        timestep equal 1e-3
timestep        ${timestep}

variable        nsteps equal 5e6
variable        Mz     equal v_E*v_I/v_R
variable        mz     equal -v_Mz*ramp(0,1)

fix             1 mobile nve/bpm/sphere update dipole
fix             2 r1 addtorque/atom 0 0 v_mz
fix             3 free gravity 0 vector 0 -1 0
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e3 12345 omega yes
fix             5 all setforce NULL NULL 0

run             $(v_nsteps)

variable        mz equal -v_Mz
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e2 12345 omega yes

run             $(14*v_nsteps)

unfix           1
unfix           2
unfix           3
unfix           4
unfix           5
undump          1

set             group all dipole 0 1 0

# write_restart   straightened-beam.restart

# =========================================================================
# Stage 1: Axial compression (80 mm)
# =========================================================================

balance         1.2 shift x 10 1.1

pair_coeff      1 1 granular hertz/material $(v_YoungsModulus) $(v_CoeffRestitution) $(v_PoissonsRatio) &
                tangential mindlin/force NULL 0.5 $(v_FrictionCoeff) &
                damping coeff_restitution

dump            1 all custom 100000 ./compress-N${N}.dump &
                id diameter x y z mux muy muz fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz c_quat[*]

variable        timestep equal 1e-5
timestep        ${timestep}

variable        nsteps equal 5e7
variable        period equal v_timestep*v_nsteps
variable        vx     equal -80/(v_timestep*v_nsteps)

fix             1 free nve/bpm/sphere update dipole
fix             2 r1 move linear ${vx} 0 0
fix             3 free gravity 9.81e-3 vector 0 -1 0
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e2 12345 omega yes

run             $(v_nsteps)

# =========================================================================
# Stage 2: Relaxation
# =========================================================================

unfix           2
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e3 12345 omega yes

run             $(4*v_nsteps)

fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e2 12345 omega yes

run             $(5*v_nsteps)

# write_restart   compressed-beam.restart

undump          1
unfix           1
unfix           3
unfix           4

# =========================================================================
# Stage 3: Twist (9 full rotations, with wall)
# =========================================================================

variable        timestep equal 1e-4
timestep        ${timestep}
reset_timestep  0

balance         1.2 shift x 10 1.1

dump            1 all custom 1000000 ./twist-N${N}.dump &
                id diameter x y z mux muy muz fx fy fz tqx tqy tqz c_quat[*]

variable        nturns       equal 9
variable        stepsperturn equal 1e8
variable        period       equal v_timestep*v_stepsperturn
variable        cycles       equal step/v_stepsperturn
variable        theta        equal 2*PI*v_cycles

fix             1 free nve/bpm/sphere update dipole
fix             2 r1 move rotate 0 0 0 -1 0 0 $(v_period) update dipole
fix             3 free gravity 9.81e-3 vector 0 -1 0
fix             4 free langevin 0 0 1e2 12345 omega yes
fix             5 free wall/gran granular &
                hertz/material $(v_YoungsModulus) $(v_CoeffRestitution) $(v_PoissonsRatio) &
                tangential mindlin/force NULL 0.5 $(v_FrictionCoeff) &
                damping coeff_restitution &
                xplane 0 220

fix             6 all print 100000 &
                "${theta} $(c_ymaxabs) $(c_zmaxabs) ${tx}" &
                file twist-N${N}-theta-yzmaxabs-Tx.txt screen no &
                title "theta [rad], y_maxabs [mm], z_maxabs [mm], Tx [uJ]"

variable        i loop ${nturns}
label           loopstart
run             $(v_stepsperturn)
next            i
jump            SELF loopstart

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Lazarus, A., Miller, J., Reis, P.M. (2013). Continuation of equilibria and stability of slender elastic rods using an asymptotic numerical method. J. Mech. Phys. Solids, 61(8), 1712–1736. doi:10.1016/j.jmps.2013.04.002
  • Lazarus, A., Miller, J.T., Metlitz, M.M., Reis, P.M. (2013). Contorting a heavy and naturally curved elastic rod. Soft Matter, 9(34), 8274–8281. doi:10.1039/c3sm50873k
  • Grange, S., Bertrand, D. (2024). Co-rotational 3D beam element using quaternion algebra. Int. J. Solids Struct., 293, 112746. doi:10.1016/j.ijsolstr.2024.112746

← Back to Examples

docs.title: Unit Systems
docs.text:
The unit systems in LAMMPS can be found here. In addition to SI units, we also use the two unit systems described below.

The cgs units assume ESU, but it can also be used for magnetostatics. The CGS EMU units are provided below. Note that there are many different conventions for Gaussian units. We use this.

docs.title: CGS EMU
docs.text:
Quantity Unit SI Length cm \(10^{-2}\) m Time s 1 s Mass g \(10^{-3}\) kg Force dyne \(10^{-5}\) N Energy erg \(10^{-7}\) J Density g/cm\(^3\) \(10^3\) kg/m\(^3\) Stress Ba \(10^{-1}\) Pa Magnetic dipole emu = erg/G \(10^{-3}\) A m\(^2\) Magnetic field G \(10^{-4}\) T Kinematic viscosity cm\(^2\)/s \(10^{-4}\) m\(^2\)/s Temperature K 1 K

A custom milli unit system (mm, mg, ms, \(\mu_0/4\pi = 1\)) is also used in some examples. All other units except temperature can be derived from the four fundamental units.

Note

The temperature in a DEM simulation is only used as a proxy for the kinetic energy of the particles, relevant only when a Langevin thermostat is applied for quasi-static equilibration. Its scale is set by \(\{k_B\}\), the numerical value of the Boltzmann constant in the chosen LAMMPS unit system (\(\{k_B\} = 1\) in units lj; \(\{k_B\} = 1.38 \times 10^{-16}\) in units cgs).

docs.title: milli
docs.text: Quantity Unit SI Length mm \(10^{-3}\) m Time ms \(10^{-3}\) s Mass mg \(10^{-6}\) kg Force mN \(10^{-3}\) N Energy \(\mu\)J \(10^{-6}\) J Density mg/mm\(^3\) \(10^3\) kg/m\(^3\) Stress kPa \(10^3\) Pa Magnetic dipole -- \(10^{-4}\) A m\(^2\) Magnetic field -- \(10^{-2}\) T Kinematic viscosity mm\(^2\)/ms \(10^{-3}\) m\(^2\)/s Temperature -- \(10^{-6}/\{k_B\}\) K
